summary: Doc-only deliverable (playbook + postmortem template + README). Fixed four references to non-existent event/checkpoint kinds that would have produced false-FAIL AC checks for operators running the soak.

findings.description: AC4 verification queried events.jsonl for kind=='daemon_started', but no daemon_started event is emitted anywhere in the codebase. The canonical 'loop started' signal is the job_started checkpoint (orchestrator/loop.py:381 -> orchestrator/checkpoint.py:99), which surfaces in events.jsonl as kind='checkpoint' with payload.checkpoint_kind='job_started'. As written, the playbook would always report 0 daemon_started events and incorrectly mark AC4 as FAIL on a successful soak. Fixed the jq query in the AC4 block and the matching evidence cell in the postmortem template.

AC4 expected completion_reason values were 'time_cap_hit | budget_cap_hit | planner_done' but daemon.py writes 'goal_complete | budget_cap | task_cap | user_stopped' (validated against ALLOWED_COMPLETION_REASONS in storage/jobs.py:47-49). Operator following the playbook would mark AC4 as FAIL on every successful run because the reason string would never match. Fixed the expected-values comment and the postmortem template's completion_reason placeholder.

Triage table referenced checkpoint_kind='final_pass_synth_started', but no such checkpoint kind exists in CHECKPOINT_KINDS (orchestrator/checkpoint.py:34-52). The actual signal for #39's post-cap final synth is an 'INFO'/'synthesis_written' event with payload.post_cap=true (orchestrator/synth.py:602-614). Fixed the jq query in the triage table.

Triage table queried 'select(.payload.kind=="source_pruned")' but source_pruned is the top-level event kind (events.py:52, emitted from disk_cap.py:295), not a payload field. The query would return zero matches even when pruning fired. Fixed to 'select(.kind=="source_pruned")'.

Wall-clock time cap is not actually enforced anywhere in orchestrator/loop.py or daemon.py. time_cap_hours is captured at intake and stored in the jobs row, but the loop only stops on goal_complete, MAX_TASKS_PER_JOB (10000-task anti-runaway), BudgetExceeded, or external STOP. A 24h soak will not self-terminate on a 24-hour wall clock. Pre-existing codebase gap (not introduced by #40); flagged inline in the playbook's AC4 comment so operators don't expect a time_cap completion_reason. Worth filing as a separate issue against orchestrator/loop or daemon.
